message:
🐛 Fix TUF and kpt missions: remove K8s workload assumptions, add correct CLI tools (#1012)

TUF: Replace kubectl/helm commands with pip/python (TUF is a Python library, not a K8s workload)
kpt: Add kpt CLI to prerequisites tools list

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,8 +11,8 @@
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Check current kpt deployment",
-        "description": "Verify your kpt version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt\nhelm list -n kpt 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kpt installation."
+        "title": "Check kpt installation",
+        "description": "Verify your kpt installation:\n```bash\nkpt version\n```\nInstall if needed: see https://kpt.dev/installation/. kpt is a Kubernetes package management CLI."
       },
       {
         "title": "Review kpt configuration",
@@ -63,7 +63,8 @@
   "prerequisites": {
     "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
     "tools": [
-      "kubectl"
+      "kubectl",
+      "kpt"
     ],
     "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kpt installed or the issue environment reproducible."
   },

@@ -11,24 +11,24 @@
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Check current the-update-framework-tuf- deployment",
-        "description": "Verify your the-update-framework-tuf- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=the-update-framework-tuf-
helm list -n the-update-framework-tuf- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working the-update-framework-tuf- installation."
+        "title": "Check python-tuf installation",
+        "description": "Verify your python-tuf installation:
```bash
pip show tuf
python3 -c "import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)"
```
Install if needed: `pip install tuf`. TUF (The Update Framework) is a Python library for secure software updates, not a Kubernetes workload."
       },
       {
-        "title": "Review the-update-framework-tuf- configuration",
-        "description": "Inspect the relevant the-update-framework-tuf- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=the-update-framework-tuf-
kubectl get configmap -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=the-update-framework-tuf-
```
**Description of issue or feature request**:

Python packaging has changed significantly since the tuf packaging was created."
+        "title": "Review the issue context",
+        "description": "**Description of issue or feature request**:

Python packaging has changed significantly since the tuf packaging was created."
       },
       {
         "title": "Apply the fix for Modernise and rationalise packaging",
         "description": "This is a WIP  trying to identify whether splitting the client and repository code and maintaining a client as
a separate installation (in a client branch for now) will be useful for client side users of tuf (e.g. pip) as well as for providing some kind of support for older versions.

Up to now I
```yaml
pip install -e ../vendoring
ERROR: File "setup.py" not found. Directory cannot be installed in editable mode: /home/jku/src/vendoring
(A "pyproject.toml" file was found, but editable mode currently requires a setup.py based build.)
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Upgrade the-update-framework-tuf- to include the fix",
-        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade the-update-framework-tuf-:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade the-update-framework-tuf- the-update-framework-tuf-/the-update-framework-tuf- --namespace the-update-framework-tuf-
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n the-update-framework-tuf-
helm list -n the-update-framework-tuf-
```"
+        "title": "Upgrade python-tuf",
+        "description": "Upgrade to the version containing the fix:
```bash
pip install --upgrade tuf
pip show tuf
```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Verify the feature works",
-        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n the-update-framework-tuf- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=the-update-framework-tuf-
kubectl get events -n the-update-framework-tuf- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Modernise and rationalise packaging" is functioning correctly."
+        "title": "Verify the fix",
+        "description": "Test that the fix is working as expected:
```bash
python3 -c "import tuf; print(tuf.__version__)"
pip show tuf
```
Confirm the feature described in "Modernise and rationalise packaging" is functioning correctly."
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
@@ -65,11 +65,11 @@
     "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
   },
   "prerequisites": {
-    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
     "tools": [
-      "kubectl"
+      "python",
+      "pip"
     ],
-    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with the-update-framework-tuf- installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+    "description": "Python 3.8+ with pip. TUF is a Python library, not a Kubernetes workload."
   },
